title: Task System Design
description:
Ongoing design and evolution of the task system. The core design is right (parent-child, worktree isolation, merge protocol), but needs polishing to truly flow.

## Open Questions

### Depth
Agents rarely go beyond 2 levels. Tree is flat, root becomes a giant flat task manager. System prompt says "keep shallow" — directly suppresses depth. Multi-level should be natural, not exceptional.

### Roles (Pinned Tasks)
No domain owners. Everything goes through root. Ideal: root manages 3-5 domain owners, tasks flow to the right person naturally. Pinned task = persistent continue + children underneath + UI prominence. No new concept needed — composition of existing capabilities.

### Progress Visibility (Partial Completion)
Task status only has passed/failed, no "partially done" semantics. yield + send_message + incremental merge already IS partial completion, but UI doesn't express it. Users can't tell where an in_progress task stands.

### Organization (Folder/Grouping)
Only tree, no pure organizational containers. Using emoji draft tasks as folder hack. Pinned task + children may be the answer — no separate folder concept needed.

### Flow
Tasks don't automatically flow to the right person. User says "fix a bug", root should identify domain, route to the right owner. Requires pinned agents as prerequisite.

### Persistent Task Sync
.mxd/tasks/*.json is source of truth but drifts from runtime. Direction: subscribe to repo, bidirectional sync.

### Agent Meeting Mode
Current communication is async parent-mediated. Meeting = direct streaming channel between agents. Deep-tree agents need direct communication.
